title: OT Cryptographic Controls and Key Management Evidence
exclusions: Excludes encryption of process-data-at-rest on legacy historians where the encryption is unavailable from the vendor (documented as compensating control). Excludes plant-floor protocols that natively do not support encryption (legacy Modbus over serial); these are mitigated via segmentation under UC-22.51.3.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.51.26` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `otcc_clause="OTCC-2-8-1-1"`. Each row preserves (asset_key, asset_class, certificate_subject, certificate_issuer, certificate_not_after, days_until_expiry, certificate_key_algorithm, certificate_key_size, certificate_signature_algorithm, certificate_expired_flag, weak_algorithm_flag, untrusted_issuer_flag, key_rotation_overdue_flag). Dashboard `NCA OTCC – OT Cryptographic Controls` shows certificate expiration heatmap and 13-month trend.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Validates X.509 certificates on OT-tier endpoints (TLS-enabled OT services, OPC UA endpoints, web-management consoles on firewalls/switches/HMI servers): expiry, key algorithm/size, signature algorithm, issuer trust, rotation. Partial because cryptographic-key management for non-X.509 keys (e.g. shared-secrets, IPsec PSKs, BACnet/SC keys) requires complementary manual attestation tracked alongside.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves OT cryptographic primitives (X.509 certificates and tracked symmetric keys) remain valid, algorithmically strong, and rotated within policy.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.51.26` archived to `audit_evidence` plus the `NCA OTCC – OT Cryptographic Controls` dashboard.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Issue a test X.509 certificate to a non-prod OT asset with `not_after` set to 25 days from now. UC-22.51.26 MUST flag the asset with `certificate_expired_flag=false` AND `days_until_expiry <= 30 AND >0` and route a 30-day warning alert.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An OT TLS endpoint with a current certificate issued by the approved internal CA, RSA-2048 or ECDSA-256, SHA-256 signature, expiring in 11 months MUST NOT be flagged.

dataSources: Certificate Transparency / internal CA event feeds (`internal_ca:event`), TLS-scan results from a scheduled scanner (Splunk-Stream TLS-scan, Tenable cert plugin, OpenSSL scan via scripted input), firewall management plane (Palo Alto Networks, Fortinet, Cisco ASA, Check Point), OT vendor management consoles. Lookups: `ot_certificate_inventory.csv`, `ot_approved_ca_list.csv`, `ot_key_rotation_policy.csv`, `ot_approved_crypto_algorithms.csv`.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Stream (Splunkbase 1809) for in-stream TLS inspection.
spl:
(index=tls_scan sourcetype="tls:scan" earliest=-7d) OR (index=ca sourcetype="internal_ca:event" earliest=-13mon action="issued")
| eval certificate_not_after_epoch = strptime(certificate_not_after, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval days_until_expiry = round((certificate_not_after_epoch - now())/86400)
| eval certificate_expired_flag = if(days_until_expiry < 0, "true", "false")
| eval certificate_expiring_soon_flag = if(days_until_expiry >= 0 AND days_until_expiry <= 30, "true", "false")
| lookup ot_approved_crypto_algorithms.csv key_algorithm AS certificate_key_algorithm OUTPUT min_key_size approved_signature_algorithms
| eval weak_algorithm_flag = if(certificate_key_size < min_key_size OR NOT match(approved_signature_algorithms, certificate_signature_algorithm), "true", "false")
| lookup ot_approved_ca_list.csv ca_subject AS certificate_issuer OUTPUT ca_trusted
| eval untrusted_issuer_flag = if(ca_trusted != "true", "true", "false")
| join asset_key type=left [search index=ca sourcetype="internal_ca:event" earliest=-13mon action="issued" | stats latest(_time) AS last_key_rotation_at BY asset_key]
| lookup ot_key_rotation_policy.csv asset_class OUTPUT key_rotation_interval_days
| eval days_since_rotation = round((now() - last_key_rotation_at)/86400)
| eval key_rotation_overdue_flag = if(days_since_rotation > key_rotation_interval_days, "true", "false")
| where certificate_expired_flag="true" OR certificate_expiring_soon_flag="true" OR weak_algorithm_flag="true" OR untrusted_issuer_flag="true" OR key_rotation_overdue_flag="true"
| table asset_key asset_class certificate_subject certificate_issuer certificate_not_after days_until_expiry certificate_key_algorithm certificate_key_size certificate_signature_algorithm certificate_expired_flag certificate_expiring_soon_flag weak_algorithm_flag untrusted_issuer_flag key_rotation_overdue_flag
| sort - certificate_expired_flag weak_algorithm_flag untrusted_issuer_flag certificate_expiring_soon_flag
description: Reconciles OT certificate inventory against expiry, algorithm strength, issuer trust, and rotation cadence; flags every breach.
value: Expired or weak OT certificates break TLS, OPC UA Sign+Encrypt, and management-plane authentication. NCA OTCC inspectors will demand cryptographic-controls evidence per asset; this UC produces it.
implementation: (1) Inventory OT certificates via scheduled TLS scans through Splunk Stream or a dedicated scanner. (2) Onboard internal CA event feeds. (3) Build `ot_approved_ca_list.csv` and `ot_approved_crypto_algorithms.csv`. (4) Build `ot_key_rotation_policy.csv`. (5) Schedule UC-22.51.26 daily.
visualization: (Panel 1) Single-value 'Expired', 'Expiring ≤30d', 'Weak algorithm', 'Untrusted issuer', 'Rotation overdue'. (Panel 2) Expiration heatmap per asset_class. (Panel 3) Sortable table. (Panel 4) 13-month trend.

references.title: NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 r5 — Recommendation for Key Management

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Self-signed certificates on legacy OT devices** with no certificate-management capability — some legacy devices issue self-signed certs that are accepted only by allow-listing fingerprints. Suppression: `self_signed_allowlisted.csv` with compensating-control attestation.

2. **Internal CA new-deployment grace** — freshly issued certificates may take time to propagate to TLS-scan systems. Suppression: 24-hour grace.

3. **OPC UA self-signed app certificates** that are trust-listed device-to-device — not all OPC UA deployments use a CA. Suppression: tag asset_class as `opcua_endpoint`; verify trust-list management via UC-22.51.6 config drift.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `otcc_22_51_26_exceptions`.
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- Splunk Enterprise Security.
- TLS-scan capability: Splunk Stream with TLS protocol parser, or a scheduled scanner (Tenable cert plugin, OpenSSL via scripted input on a scan host).
- Internal CA event feed (Microsoft ADCS, OpenSSL CA, EJBCA, AWS Private CA).
- `index=tls_scan` (24-month retention), `index=ca`, `index=audit_evidence`.
- Lookups:
  - `ot_approved_ca_list.csv` — owned by Head of OT Security. Columns: `ca_subject`, `ca_trusted` (boolean), `ca_purpose`. Includes internal-CA subjects plus any approved external (DigiCert, GlobalSign).
  - `ot_approved_crypto_algorithms.csv` — owned by Head of OT Security. Columns: `key_algorithm`, `min_key_size`, `approved_signature_algorithms` (mvfield). Examples:

    ```csv
    key_algorithm,min_key_size,approved_signature_algorithms
    RSA,2048,"sha256WithRSAEncryption|sha384WithRSAEncryption|sha512WithRSAEncryption"
    EC,256,"ecdsa-with-SHA256|ecdsa-with-SHA384|ecdsa-with-SHA512"
    ```

  - `ot_key_rotation_policy.csv` — columns: `asset_class`, `key_rotation_interval_days`. Examples:

    ```csv
    asset_class,key_rotation_interval_days
    firewall_ot_dmz,365
    plc_controller,730
    hmi_windows,365
    opcua_endpoint,365
    engineering_workstation_windows,365
    sis_logic_solver,1825
    ```

  - KV Store `otcc_22_51_26_exceptions`.

## Step 1 — Configure TLS scanning

For in-stream visibility via Splunk Stream:

```ini
[streamfwd]
ipAddr = 0.0.0.0
deployment_url = https://stream.example.com
stream_filter = tls
index = tls_scan
```

The TLS metadata sourcetype `stream:tls` includes `cert_subject`, `cert_issuer`, `cert_not_after`, `cert_key_algorithm`, `cert_key_size`, `cert_signature_algorithm`.

For active scanning (some OT segments do not have Stream coverage), schedule a scripted-input scanner that lists every asset in `ot_asset_inventory_lookup` with `tls_listener_port` populated and emits scan events.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Flags per SPL.

**Schedule**:

```ini
[UC-22.51.26: OT Cryptographic Controls and Key Management Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 9 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -7d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, email
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.51.26,reg=NCA_OTCC,clause=OTCC-2-8-1-1
action.email.to = ot-cyber-engineering@example.com,ot-soc@example.com
alert.severity = 3
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a)** TLS-scan covers every OT asset with a TLS listener.

**(b)** Approved CA list and crypto algorithm list current.

**(c)** Positive control per `controlTest.positiveScenario`.

**(d)** Negative control per `controlTest.negativeScenario`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `NCA OTCC – OT Cryptographic Controls`.

- **Row 1, single values**: 5 flags.
- **Row 2, heatmap**: expiry by asset_class.
- **Row 3, sortable table** of flagged assets with drill-through.
- **Row 4, 13-month trend**.

**Runbook for `certificate_expiring_soon_flag=true`** (≤30 days): P3 to OT cyber-engineering. Renew via internal CA per change-control UC-22.51.9. Re-deploy to the OT asset. Confirm on next scan.

**Runbook for `weak_algorithm_flag=true`** (e.g. MD5 / SHA-1 / RSA-1024 / DSA): P2. Often associated with very legacy devices. If vendor offers no upgrade, document compensating control (network isolation, MFA at PSM) in `audit_evidence`.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**TLS-scan dark spots** — some OT segments are isolated; deploy a scanner inside each L3.5 DMZ.

**OT-specific failure modes**:

- **OPC UA application certificates**: device-to-device trust-listed; not always issued by a central CA. Track separately if your deployment uses GDS (Global Discovery Server) for certificate management.
- **Vendor-managed certificate stores** (Rockwell, Schneider firmware-embedded): the certificate cannot be re-issued without firmware update. Document compensating controls.
- **PLC HTTPS interfaces with self-signed certs**: many controllers do not support external CAs; mitigate via management-plane segmentation (UC-22.51.3) and rotate self-signed certs on a defined cadence (UC-22.51.6).
- **Plant-floor protocols without crypto** (legacy Modbus, DNP3 without secure-auth): isolation under UC-22.51.3 plus PA per UC-22.51.18.
grandmaExplanation: We check that all the digital keys and certificates used to lock up plant equipment are still good, strong, and from a source we trust. If any are about to expire, weak, or strange, we put it in the to-do list.
equipmentModels: AWS Key Management Service / Azure Key Vault / GCP Cloud KMS (CMK lifecycle events)
HashiCorp Vault Enterprise (transit and PKI secrets engines)
Splunk Stream Independent Forwarder (TLS metadata for cipher / version inspection)
Splunk Add-on for AWS / Microsoft Azure / Google Cloud (Splunkbase 1876 / 3110 / 5556)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, Authentication and Network_Traffic data models)
